messageValues.message: You will receive a JSON array of links from a business website.  First, remove any links that are sitemaps, XML files, RSS feeds, legal pages, privacy  policies, duplicate pages, blog categories, or any technical or utility URLs that  contain no business content.  If the remaining links are 10 or fewer, return them all.  If the remaining links are more than 10, keep only the most strategically valuable  ones that together give a complete picture of what the business does, who it serves,  and how to contact them. Maximum 10 links.  Respond only with a valid JSON array of the kept link objects in the exact same format  you received them. Nothing else. No explanation. No markdown.

options.systemMessage:
## Role

You are a cold outreach copywriter for Victor...

## Role

You are a follow-up message writer...

parameters.content:
## 🤖 AI-Powered Cold Outreach Automation

Automate the full cold email lifecycle — from crawling a prospect's website to writing a personalised email and managing follow-ups.

### 📌 Who is this for?
Freelancers, agencies, and sales teams doing outbound outreach at scale who want personalised emails without manual research.

### ⚡ What it does
**Cold Email Branch** (runs first):
1. Reads **pending** leads from Google Sheets
2. Crawls each lead's website to understand their business
3. AI writes a tailored cold email *(subject + body + contact email)*
4. Email is verified; Gmail draft is created
5. Sheet updated to `status = emailed`

**Follow-Up Branch** (runs after cold emails):
1. Reads leads with `status = emailed` and fewer than 5 follow-ups
2. Fetches the full Gmail thread for context
3. AI writes a context-aware reply in the same thread
4. Sheet increments `follow_ups` count

### 🔧 Credentials Required
| Credential | Used For |
|---|---|
| Google Sheets OAuth2 | Read/update lead database |
| Firecrawl API | Sitemap crawl + batch scrape + agent |
| Groq API | Fast URL filtering |
| Google Gemini API | Email generation (both branches) |
| Gmail OAuth2 | Create drafts, fetch threads |
| VerifiEmail API | Validate email addresses |

### 📋 Sheet Column Reference
`domain` · `niche` · `region` · `email` · `status` · `follow_ups`

## 🚀 Setup Checklist

**Step 1 — Google Sheets**
Create a sheet with columns:
`domain` | `niche` | `region` | `email` | `status` | `follow_ups`
Set new leads to `status = pending`, `follow_ups = 0`

**Step 2 — Add All Credentials**
Add all 6 credential types in *Settings → Credentials* before running.

**Step 3 — Customise the AI Prompts**
Open **"✍️ Write Cold Email"** → edit the system prompt with:
- Your name / company
- Your offer / value proposition
- Desired tone and length

Open **"✍️ Write Follow-Up Email"** → same customisation.

**Step 4 — Point to Your Sheet**
Update the `documentId` in any Google Sheets node to your own spreadsheet ID.

**Step 5 — Test With One Lead**
Add a single row with `status = pending` and run manually to verify end-to-end.

---

**Status Values**
| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `pending` | New, not yet contacted |
| `emailed` | Cold email drafted |
| `no_email` | No valid email found — skip |
| `unsubscribed` | Do not contact |

## 📥 Trigger & Lead Intake
Reads every row from the scouting database, filters down to leads eligible for a cold email *(status = pending, follow_ups = 0, not unsubscribed)*, then loops through them one at a time.

## 🕷️ Website Intelligence Pipeline
Crawls the prospect's website to extract business context. Uses Firecrawl's sitemap API to discover all pages, then AI filters them down to the 10 most strategically useful, then batch-scrapes them in parallel.

## ⏳ Async Scrape Polling — Standard (SplitInBatches)
Firecrawl batch scrapes are **asynchronous** — the API returns a job ID, not results.

This loop polls every 5 seconds using a classic n8n pattern:
`SplitInBatches → Get Status → IF check → Wait → Set (re-attach ID) → loop`

Advantage: **built-in nodes only** — works on any n8n instance without extra installs.

💡 See **Version B** (right) for a cleaner 2-node alternative using the Do...While community node.

## ✍️ AI Cold Email Generation
Aggregates all scraped page markdown into a single payload and passes it to a Gemini-powered AI agent. The agent analyses the business and produces a JSON object with `subject`, `body`, and the best contact `email` it found in the content.

## ✅ Email Verification & Gmail Draft
Gates email sending behind two checks:
1. **Did the AI find an email?** If not, the Firecrawl Agent actively hunts for one on the site.
2. **Is the email real?** VerifiEmail confirms deliverability before any draft is created.
If no valid email can be found after both attempts, the lead is marked `no_email` so it's **never retried**, preventing wasted API calls on future runs.

## 🔁 Follow-Up Pipeline
Runs automatically after cold emails. For each lead with `status = emailed` and fewer than 5 follow-ups: fetches the full Gmail conversation, summarises it with memory-aware AI, and drafts a context-appropriate reply in the same thread. The `follow_ups` counter increments each time.

**🔄 Sequential by design**
When this loop finishes all cold emails *(output 0 — done)*, it automatically triggers the follow-up pipeline. Cold emails always run before follow-ups in a single execution.

**⚡ Why Groq here?**
URL filtering is a fast, simple task — it just cuts a list. Groq's ultra-low latency makes this step near-instant and cheap. Gemini is reserved for creative writing further down.

**⏱️ Why poll instead of a fixed wait?**
A fixed wait would be too short for large sites or waste time on small ones. This polling pattern adapts dynamically to actual scrape duration.

**🧠 Two LLMs, two jobs**
**Groq** (fast, cheap) → URL filtering — structured list task
**Gemini** (capable, creative) → Email writing — creative task
Swap either for your preferred provider in Credentials.

**🔍 Agent fallback — why it's here**
The cold email AI sometimes can't find a contact email from scraped page content alone. The Firecrawl AI Agent is a second-pass tool: it actively navigates the site (contact pages, footers, mailto links) specifically to find an email.

**🚫 Why mark no_email?**
Without this status update, leads with no findable email would be retried on every single run — burning Firecrawl and AI credits for zero outcome.

**💾 Per-contact memory**
Session memory is keyed to each lead's email address. This lets the AI track what was said across previous follow-up runs and avoid repeating itself.

First, remove any links that are sitemaps, XML files, RSS feeds, legal pages, privacy  policies, duplicate pages, blog categories, or any technical or utility URLs that  contain no business content.  If the remaining links are 10 or fewer, return them all.  If the remaining links are more than 10, keep only the most strategically valuable  ones that together give a complete picture of what the business does, who it serves,  and how to contact them. Maximum 10 links.  Respond only with a valid JSON array of the kept link objects in the exact same format  you received them. Nothing else. No explanation. No markdown.

parameters.content:
## 🤖 AI-Powered Cold Outreach Automation

Automate the full cold email lifecycle — from crawling a prospect's website to writing a personalised email and managing follow-ups.

### 📌 Who is this for?
Freelancers, agencies, and sales teams doing outbound outreach at scale who want personalised emails without manual research.

### ⚡ What it does
**Cold Email Branch** (runs first):
1. Reads **pending** leads from Google Sheets
2. Crawls each lead's website to understand their business
3. AI writes a tailored cold email *(subject + body + contact email)*
4. Email is verified; Gmail draft is created
5. Sheet updated to `status = emailed`

**Follow-Up Branch** (runs after cold emails):
1. Reads leads with `status = emailed` and fewer than 5 follow-ups
2. Fetches the full Gmail thread for context
3. AI writes a context-aware reply in the same thread
4. Sheet increments `follow_ups` count

### 🔧 Credentials Required
| Credential | Used For |
|---|---|
| Google Sheets OAuth2 | Read/update lead database |
| Firecrawl API | Sitemap crawl + batch scrape + agent |
| Groq API | Fast URL filtering |
| Google Gemini API | Email generation (both branches) |
| Gmail OAuth2 | Create drafts, fetch threads |
| VerifiEmail API | Validate email addresses |

### 📋 Sheet Column Reference
`domain` · `niche` · `region` · `email` · `status` · `follow_ups`

## 🚀 Setup Checklist

**Step 1 — Google Sheets**
Create a sheet with columns:
`domain` | `niche` | `region` | `email` | `status` | `follow_ups`
Set new leads to `status = pending`, `follow_ups = 0`

**Step 2 — Add All Credentials**
Add all 6 credential types in *Settings → Credentials* before running.

**Step 3 — Customise the AI Prompts**
Open **"✍️ Write Cold Email"** → edit the system prompt with:
- Your name / company
- Your offer / value proposition
- Desired tone and length

Open **"✍️ Write Follow-Up Email"** → same customisation.

**Step 4 — Point to Your Sheet**
Update the `documentId` in any Google Sheets node to your own spreadsheet ID.

**Step 5 — Test With One Lead**
Add a single row with `status = pending` and run manually to verify end-to-end.

---

**Status Values**
| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `pending` | New, not yet contacted |
| `emailed` | Cold email drafted |
| `no_email` | No valid email found — skip |
| `unsubscribed` | Do not contact |

## 📥 Trigger & Lead Intake
Reads every row from the scouting database, filters down to leads eligible for a cold email *(status = pending, follow_ups = 0, not unsubscribed)*, then loops through them one at a time.

## 🕷️ Website Intelligence Pipeline
Crawls the prospect's website to extract business context. Uses Firecrawl's sitemap API to discover all pages, then AI filters them down to the 10 most strategically useful, then batch-scrapes them in parallel.

## ⏳ Async Scrape Polling — Do...While (Community Node)
Firecrawl batch scrapes are **asynchronous** — the API returns a job ID, not results.

This version uses the `n8n-nodes-do-while` community node to replace **5 nodes** with just **2**:

| Old pattern (Version A) | This version (Version B) |
|---|---|
| SplitInBatches | ✅ Do...While |
| Get Crawl Status | ✅ Get Crawl Status |
| IF (still scraping?) | ~~replaced~~ |
| Wait 5s | ~~replaced~~ |
| Set (re-attach ID) | ~~replaced~~ |

**Parameters used:**
- `condition`: `$json.data?.status !== 'scraping'` — exits when done
- `waitSeconds: 5` — built-in delay between polls
- `maxIterations: 30` — 2.5 min hard cap, then exits gracefully
- `maxBehavior: exit` — no error thrown if cap is hit

⚙️ **Install first:** Settings → Community Nodes → `n8n-nodes-do-while`

## ✍️ AI Cold Email Generation
Aggregates all scraped page markdown into a single payload and passes it to a Gemini-powered AI agent. The agent analyses the business and produces a JSON object with `subject`, `body`, and the best contact `email` it found in the content.

## ✅ Email Verification & Gmail Draft
Gates email sending behind two checks:
1. **Did the AI find an email?** If not, the Firecrawl Agent actively hunts for one on the site.
2. **Is the email real?** VerifiEmail confirms deliverability before any draft is created.
If no valid email can be found after both attempts, the lead is marked `no_email` so it's **never retried**, preventing wasted API calls on future runs.

## 🔁 Follow-Up Pipeline
Runs automatically after cold emails. For each lead with `status = emailed` and fewer than 5 follow-ups: fetches the full Gmail conversation, summarises it with memory-aware AI, and drafts a context-appropriate reply in the same thread. The `follow_ups` counter increments each time.

**🔄 Sequential by design**
When this loop finishes all cold emails *(output 0 — done)*, it automatically triggers the follow-up pipeline. Cold emails always run before follow-ups in a single execution.

**⚡ Why Groq here?**
URL filtering is a fast, simple task — it just cuts a list. Groq's ultra-low latency makes this step near-instant and cheap. Gemini is reserved for creative writing further down.

**⏱️ Why poll instead of a fixed wait?**
A fixed wait would be too short for large sites or waste time on small ones. This polling pattern adapts dynamically to actual scrape duration.

**🧠 Two LLMs, two jobs**
**Groq** (fast, cheap) → URL filtering — structured list task
**Gemini** (capable, creative) → Email writing — creative task
Swap either for your preferred provider in Credentials.

**🔍 Agent fallback — why it's here**
The cold email AI sometimes can't find a contact email from scraped page content alone. The Firecrawl AI Agent is a second-pass tool: it actively navigates the site (contact pages, footers, mailto links) specifically to find an email.

**🚫 Why mark no_email?**
Without this status update, leads with no findable email would be retried on every single run — burning Firecrawl and AI credits for zero outcome.

**💾 Per-contact memory**
Session memory is keyed to each lead's email address. This lets the AI track what was said across previous follow-up runs and avoid repeating itself.

# 🔀 Two Versions — Choose One, Delete the Other

This canvas contains **two complete, fully working versions** of the same cold outreach workflow.
They are **identical** except for how the async scrape polling loop is built.

---

### 🅰️ Version A — Standard (left) · Built-in nodes only
Uses `SplitInBatches + IF + Wait + Set` to poll the Firecrawl scrape status.
✅ **No extra installs.** Works on n8n Cloud and any self-hosted instance out of the box.

### 🅱️ Version B — Do...While (right) · Community node required
Uses the `n8n-nodes-do-while` community node to replace those 5 nodes with 2.
Cleaner canvas, built-in wait, built-in iteration cap, and easier to reason about.
⚠️ **Requires installation:** Settings → Community Nodes → install `n8n-nodes-do-while`

---

**To use Version A:** delete all nodes labelled `[v2]` on the right.
**To use Version B:** delete all nodes on the left that are NOT labelled `[v2]`, then remove the `[v2]` suffix from node names.

## 🅰️ Version A
### Standard · Built-in Nodes Only
No extra installs required.

## 🅱️ Version B
### Do...While · Community Node
Install `n8n-nodes-do-while` first.
Settings → Community Nodes → Install
